rationale.ARI: Most-cited spatial-clustering metric; biased toward fewer clusters, kept for community comparability.
rationale.AMI: Chance-corrected mutual information; the de-biased ARI/NMI replacement. ADR 0011 prefers AMI for the stability axis.
rationale.V_measure: Harmonic mean of homogeneity + completeness; exposes whether failures are over-merge (low H) vs over-split (low C). H and C report-only for diagnosis.
rationale.MLAMI: Multi-scale spatial coherence via Leiden-AMI on the spatial k-NN graph (ported from nichecompass BSD-3). Unsupervised; complements GT-comparing metrics.
rationale.noise_floor: consensus_metric ≥ best_member_metric - noise_floor — consensus must be no worse than the best single method within the published-noise band.
rationale.min_absolute: Hard lower bound — if every method does this badly something is broken upstream (preprocessing, ground truth file, alignment).
rationale.min_members_surviving: ADR 0010 requires ≥2 globally; this benchmark requires ≥4 of the 5 to confidently report a consensus-quality regression.
rationale.CHAOS_PAS: 1-hop spatial label-agreement aggregates (SACCELERATOR-equivalent). Report-only diagnostic — useful for spotting over-clustered or salt-and-pepper outputs.

source_reference: Maynard, Collado-Torres et al. 2021. Nat Neurosci 24(3):425-436. doi:10.1038/s41593-020-00787-0

ported_metric_attribution.MLAMI: Adapted from nichecompass v1.x (Birk, Talavera-López, Lotfollahi; BSD 3-Clause, 2024) src/nichecompass/benchmarking/mlami.py
ported_metric_attribution.CHAOS_PAS: Python equivalents of SACCELERATOR (MIT-0) consensus/02_Smoothness_entropy family
